ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Order created successfully via UI when order date, customer, and one line item are all provided
classification_rationale: Validates the single feature requirement that all required fields must be present to successfully create an order.
preconditions: Order Management application is deployed and accessible in UAT environment at https://orders.uat.example.com
User account sales.user01 exists with role Sales_Representative and has Create permission on Order Management module
Customer record CUST-10045 (Acme Industries) exists in Active status in the customer database
Product SKU-90210 (Wireless Keyboard) exists with unit price $49.99 and is in stock
test_data: Line Item 1 - Product: SKU-90210 (Wireless Keyboard), Quantity: 2, Unit Price: $49.99
steps: Navigate to the Create Order page from the Orders menu
Enter 2025-07-15 in the Order Date field
Search for and select customer CUST-10045 (Acme Industries) in the Customer field
Click Add Line Item button
Enter SKU-90210 in the Product field of the first line item row
Enter 2 in the Quantity field of the first line item row
Click the Submit Order button
expected_result: Order is created successfully. A confirmation message displays 'Order created successfully' with a system-generated Order ID in format ORD-XXXXXXX. The order summary shows Order Date as 2025-07-15, Customer as Acme Industries (CUST-10045), and one line item for SKU-90210 with quantity 2 and line total $99.98. Order status is set to Created.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.

classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path business rule at the API layer ensuring all required fields produce a successful order creation response.

Product SKU-44321 (USB-C Hub) exists with unit price $34.50 and is in stock

expected_result: HTTP 201 Created is returned. Response body contains a generated order_id matching pattern ORD-\d{7}, order_date as 2025-07-16, customer_id as CUST-20078, status as Created, and a line_items array with one entry for SKU-44321 with quantity 5 and line_total 172.50.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Blank order date, blank customer, and no line items shows inline validation errors in UI

preconditions: Order Management application is deployed and accessible in UAT environment at https://orders.uat.example.com
User account sales.user01 exists with role Sales_Representative and has Create permission on Order Management module
steps: Navigate to the Create Order page from the Orders menu
Leave the Order Date field empty
Leave the Customer field empty
Do not add any line items
Click the Submit Order button
expected_result: Order is not created. Submit Order button action is blocked or returns validation errors. Three inline validation messages display: 'Order Date is required' beneath the Order Date field, 'Customer is required' beneath the Customer field, and 'At least one line item is required' in the Line Items section. No success confirmation or order ID is generated.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: Unauthenticated request to create order API endpoint is rejected with 401
classification_rationale: Non-functional security test validating that the order creation endpoint enforces authentication before processing any required-field logic.
preconditions: Order Management API is available at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders
No valid authentication token is provided in the request
steps: Send a POST request to /v1/orders with a valid JSON body containing all required fields but without an Authorization header
expected_result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned. Response body contains error message 'Authentication required'. No order is created and no order_id is present in the response.

ac_text: Required fields order date, customer, and at least one line item must be present.
title: No partial order record persists in database when required field validation fails
classification_rationale: Validates data integrity by confirming no orphan or partial order rows are written when a required-field validation error occurs.
preconditions: Order Management API is available at https://api.uat.example.com/v1/orders
Direct read access is available to the orders database schema on db-uat.example.com, database order_mgmt
Valid OAuth2 bearer token exists for service account svc-sales-api with scope orders:create
Current maximum order_id in orders table is ORD-0050000
Product SKU-44321 (USB-C Hub) exists with unit price $34.50
test_data: SQL verification query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_date = '2025-07-17' AND customer_id IS NULL AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes'
steps: Send a POST request to /v1/orders with the JSON body containing order_date 2025-07-17 and one line item but omitting customer_id
Confirm the API returns HTTP 400
Execute the SQL query against orders table: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE order_date = '2025-07-17' AND customer_id IS NULL AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes'
Execute the SQL query against order_line_items table: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id > 'ORD-0050000' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes'
expected_result: Both SQL queries return a count of 0. No row exists in the orders table with a NULL customer_id for order_date 2025-07-17. No orphan rows exist in order_line_items for any order_id beyond ORD-0050000 created in the last 5 minutes. Data integrity is maintained.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Invalid product codes must be rejected.
title: Database does not persist any order or line item record when an invalid product code is submitted
classification_rationale: Validates at the database layer that rejection of an invalid product code results in zero persisted records, ensuring transactional integrity.
preconditions: Database is accessible in UAT environment with read access for QA verification.
Product table does NOT contain a row with product_code = 'FAKE-5555'.
Customer CUST-10045 exists in the customers table with status = 'Active'.
Current maximum order_id in the orders table is noted as baseline (e.g., ORD-98765).
test_data: SQL Verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 'CUST-10045' AND order_date = '2025-07-15' AND order_id > 'ORD-98765'
SQL Verification: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_line_items WHERE product_code = 'FAKE-5555'
steps: Send a POST request to /api/v1/orders with the specified request body containing invalid product code FAKE-5555.
Execute the SQL query against the orders table to count new orders for CUST-10045 after baseline order_id ORD-98765.
Execute the SQL query against the order_line_items table to count rows with product_code FAKE-5555.
expected_result: The orders table query returns count 0 — no new order record is created. The order_line_items table query returns count 0 — no line item with product_code FAKE-5555 exists. Database transactional rollback is confirmed.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Invalid product codes must be rejected.

classification_rationale: Validates that the ETL ingestion layer correctly identifies and quarantines records with invalid product codes from upstream source feeds.
preconditions: ETL pipeline for order ingestion is deployed and scheduled in UAT environment.
Product reference table in the data warehouse does NOT contain product_code DEL-8888.
Source staging area is accessible and writable for test file placement.
Quarantine/error table (etl_order_errors) is empty or baseline count is recorded.
test_data: File Row: ORDER_ID=ETL-ORD-501, ORDER_DATE=2025-07-15, CUSTOMER_ID=CUST-10045, PRODUCT_CODE=DEL-8888, QUANTITY=10
steps: Place the file orders_feed_20250715.csv containing the row with product code DEL-8888 into the ETL source staging directory.
Trigger the order ingestion ETL pipeline execution.
Wait for the pipeline run to complete.
Query the etl_order_errors quarantine table for records with source_order_id = 'ETL-ORD-501'.
Query the orders target table for records with source_order_id = 'ETL-ORD-501'.
expected_result: The etl_order_errors table contains one record with source_order_id ETL-ORD-501, error_code ERR_INVALID_PRODUCT, and error_message referencing product code DEL-8888. The orders target table contains zero records for source_order_id ETL-ORD-501. The ETL pipeline run completes with status 'Completed with errors'.

Select customer CUST-10045 (Acme Industries) from the customer dropdown.
Add line item with product SKU-20010, quantity 2.

ac_text: Tax should be calculated for taxable products.
title: Database stores correct tax_amount, tax_rate, and taxable flag after order creation with taxable product
classification_rationale: Validates that the calculated tax values are persisted correctly at the database layer for audit and downstream consumption.
preconditions: Database is accessible with read-only query account 'qa_db_reader' on schema 'order_mgmt'.

An order has been successfully created via API with order_id ORD-78501 for customer CUST-10045, containing product SKU-20010 (taxable, unit price $49.99), quantity 2, in Texas jurisdiction (8.25% tax rate).
Expected persisted tax_amount is $8.25 and subtotal is $99.98.
test_data: Query 1: SELECT order_id, subtotal, tax_amount, tax_rate, total FROM orders WHERE order_id = 'ORD-78501'
Query 2: SELECT order_id, product_code, quantity, unit_price, is_taxable, line_tax FROM order_line_items WHERE order_id = 'ORD-78501'
Expected orders row: subtotal = 99.98, tax_amount = 8.25, tax_rate = 0.0825, total = 108.23
Expected order_line_items row: product_code = 'SKU-20010', quantity = 2, unit_price = 49.99, is_taxable = true, line_tax = 8.25
steps: Execute Query 1 against the order_mgmt.orders table.
Execute Query 2 against the order_mgmt.order_line_items table.
expected_result: Query 1 returns exactly one row with subtotal = 99.98, tax_amount = 8.25, tax_rate = 0.0825, and total = 108.23. Query 2 returns one row with product_code 'SKU-20010', is_taxable = true, and line_tax = 8.25. All monetary values are stored as DECIMAL(10,2).
review_feedback: completeness: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Missing execution detail or contains quality violations

ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.
title: Order created via UI triggers downstream integration event visible in event log
classification_rationale: Validates the happy-path flow from the UI perspective ensuring the order creation event is emitted after a user submits an order through the interface.
preconditions: Order Management web application is accessible at https://orders.example.com
User account sales_user_01 exists with role Sales_Agent and has create permission on Order module
User sales_user_01 is authenticated and the Create Order page is loaded
Customer CUST-20078 (Beta Industries) exists with active status
Product SKU-B2002 (Gadget Lite) exists with unit price $49.99 and is in stock
Integration event monitoring dashboard is accessible at https://monitor.example.com/events
test_data: Line Item: SKU-B2002, Quantity: 1, Unit Price: $49.99
steps: Select customer CUST-20078 (Beta Industries) from the Customer dropdown
Set the Order Date field to 2025-02-10
Click the Add Line Item button
Enter product code SKU-B2002 in the Product Code field
Enter 1 in the Quantity field
Click the Submit Order button
Navigate to the integration event monitoring dashboard at https://monitor.example.com/events
Filter events by event type 'order.created' and the displayed order ID
expected_result: Order confirmation page displays with a new order ID and status 'Created'. The integration event monitoring dashboard shows an 'order.created' event for the new order ID with customer_id CUST-20078, order_date 2025-02-10, product_code SKU-B2002, quantity 1, and a timestamp within the last 60 seconds.

ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.
title: Order creation via API publishes event to message broker with correct order payload

preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.example.com/v1/orders

User account exists with role Sales_Agent and has create permission on Order module, using API key: APIKEY-SA-77201
Customer CUST-10045 (Acme Corp) exists in the system with active status
Product SKU-A1001 (Widget Pro) exists with unit price $25.00 and is in stock

steps: Send POST request to https://api.example.com/v1/orders with the order payload containing customer_id CUST-10045, order_date 2025-01-15, and one line item for SKU-A1001 quantity 3
Capture the HTTP response and extract the order_id from the response body
Poll the message broker test harness on topic 'order.created' for up to 10 seconds for a message matching the returned order_id
expected_result: HTTP 201 is returned with a generated order_id. Within 10 seconds, a message appears on topic 'order.created' containing order_id, customer_id CUST-10045, order_date 2025-01-15, line_items with product_code SKU-A1001, quantity 3, unit_price 25.00, and an event_timestamp in ISO-8601 format.

Query om.order_events_outbox WHERE order_id = 'ORD-99501'
expected_result: Exactly one row exists in om.order_events_outbox with order_id ORD-99501, event_type 'order.created', status 'PENDING', and a JSON payload containing customer_id CUST-10045, order_date 2025-04-20, and line_items array with SKU-A1001, quantity 10, unit_price 25.00. The created_at timestamp is within the last 60 seconds.

Wait up to 30 seconds for the ETL pipeline 'order-event-ingest' to process the message

expected_result: A row exists in dw_stage.order_events with source_order_id ORD-88100, customer_id CUST-30099, order_date 2025-03-01, line_item_count 2, total_amount 90.00, and event_status 'PROCESSED'. ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.
title: Unauthorised API request to create order does not emit any integration event
classification_rationale: Security-focused test validating that unauthenticated order creation attempts are rejected and no spurious events reach downstream integrations.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.example.com/v1/orders
Message broker topic 'order.created' test harness consumer is subscribed with baseline message count recorded
No valid authentication token is provided for the request

Wait 10 seconds
Check the message broker test harness on topic 'order.created' for any new messages since the baseline count
expected_result: HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned. Response body contains error message 'Authentication required' or 'Invalid token'. No new message appears on topic 'order.created' — the message count remains at the baseline. No order record is created in the system.

ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.

classification_rationale: Validates the ETL layer's rejection behavior when an inbound order event is missing required fields, ensuring data quality for downstream consumers.

Error logging table dw_stage.etl_error_log is accessible

Wait up to 30 seconds for ETL pipeline processing
Query dw_stage.order_events WHERE source_order_id = 'ORD-88200'
Query dw_stage.etl_error_log WHERE source_order_id = 'ORD-88200'
Check dead-letter queue 'order.created.dlq' for message with order_id ORD-88200
expected_result: No row exists in dw_stage.order_events for source_order_id ORD-88200. A row exists in dw_stage.etl_error_log with source_order_id ORD-88200, error_message containing 'customer_id is required', and error_timestamp within the last 60 seconds. The malformed event is present on the dead-letter queue 'order.created.dlq'.

Capture the HTTP response and extract the order_id
Poll the message broker test harness on topic 'order.created' for up to 15 seconds for a message matching the returned order_id
Parse the event payload and count the line_items array length
expected_result: HTTP 201 is returned with a valid order_id. An 'order.created' event appears on the broker topic within 15 seconds. The event payload contains customer_id CUST-40010, order_date 2025-06-01, and a line_items array with exactly 50 elements, each containing the correct product_code, quantity 1, and unit_price 5.00. ac_text: Order creation event must be available to downstream integrations.
title: Downstream message broker outage causes order event to be retried and eventually delivered
classification_rationale: Validates the failure recovery mechanism ensuring order creation events are not lost when the downstream integration target is temporarily unavailable.
preconditions: Order Management API is deployed and accessible
Message broker consumer for topic 'order.created' is temporarily stopped to simulate downstream outage
Outbox relay service is configured with retry policy: max 3 retries, 5-second interval
Table om.order_events_outbox is accessible for monitoring
Customer CUST-10045 and product SKU-A1001 exist in the system
test_data: Expected outbox statuses: PENDING → RETRY → DELIVERED
steps: Send POST request to https://api.example.com/v1/orders with customer_id CUST-10045, order_date 2025-05-01, line item SKU-A1001 quantity 2
Query om.order_events_outbox WHERE order_id = 'ORD-99601' and verify status is PENDING or RETRY
Wait 10 seconds to allow at least one retry attempt while broker consumer is stopped
Query om.order_events_outbox WHERE order_id = 'ORD-99601' to confirm retry_count has incremented
Restart the message broker consumer for topic 'order.created'
Wait up to 20 seconds for the outbox relay to successfully deliver the event
Query om.order_events_outbox WHERE order_id = 'ORD-99601' for final status
expected_result: Order is created with HTTP 201. The outbox row for ORD-99601 shows retry_count >= 1 during the outage window. After the broker consumer is restored, the outbox status transitions to 'DELIVERED'. The 'order.created' event with order_id ORD-99601, customer_id CUST-10045, and correct line item data appears on topic 'order.created'.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: 1 step(s) contain assertions — validations must go only in Expected Results; Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Cancelled orders cannot move to shipped.
title: Rapid consecutive API calls to ship a cancelled order are all rejected without race condition
classification_rationale: Validates the business rule holds under concurrent request conditions, which is a boundary scenario for single-feature enforcement.
preconditions: Order ORD-50216 exists in the database with status 'Cancelled'.
API authentication token is valid for user ops_api_user with role Operations_Manager.
Order Management API is deployed and accessible at https://api.example.com/v1/orders.
steps: Send 5 simultaneous PUT requests to https://api.example.com/v1/orders/ORD-50216/status with body {"status": "Shipped"} using valid authorization header.
Collect all 5 response codes and response bodies.
Query the orders table for order ORD-50216.
expected_result: All 5 responses return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity with error message 'Cancelled orders cannot be transitioned to Shipped.' The orders table shows status 'Cancelled' for ORD-50216. No audit log entry records a successful transition to 'Shipped'.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Order status change from Processing to Shipped is visible in Order Status Report and triggers email notification
classification_rationale: Validates the core business rule that a status update appears in both reporting and notifications within the Order Management module.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0071 exists with current status 'Processing' for customer CUST-30012 (Acme Corp)
User account ops_user_jdoe exists with role Operations_Manager and has update permission on Order Status module
Email notification configuration is active for order status changes with recipient ops_notify@acmecorp.com
Order Status Report is accessible at /reports/order-status and includes order ORD-20250-0071
steps: Navigate to the Order Detail page for order ORD-20250-0071
Select 'Shipped' from the Status dropdown
Click the 'Update Status' button
Navigate to the Order Status Report at /reports/order-status
Filter the report by Order ID ORD-20250-0071
Open the email inbox for ops_notify@acmecorp.com
expected_result: Order ORD-20250-0071 displays status 'Shipped' on the Order Detail page. The Order Status Report shows a row for ORD-20250-0071 with status 'Shipped', updated by ops_user_jdoe, and timestamp 2025-07-15T10:30:00Z. An email notification is delivered to ops_notify@acmecorp.com with subject 'Order ORD-20250-0071 Status Updated to Shipped' containing the order ID, new status, and timestamp.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: API status update to Delivered propagates to reporting data and triggers notification event
classification_rationale: Validates the same AC via the API layer to ensure programmatic status updates also appear in reporting and notifications.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0088 exists with current status 'Shipped' for customer CUST-30045 (Beta Industries)
API authentication token is valid for service account svc_ops_api with scope order:update
Notification service is running and subscribed to order.status.updated events
Reporting data warehouse is connected and consuming order status change events
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250-0088/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/order-status?orderId=ORD-20250-0088
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?orderId=ORD-20250-0088&event=order.status.updated
expected_result: PUT response returns HTTP 200 with body {"orderId": "ORD-20250-0088", "status": "Delivered", "previousStatus": "Shipped"}. GET /reports/order-status returns a record for ORD-20250-0088 with status 'Delivered' and timestamp 2025-07-15T14:00:00Z. GET /notifications returns at least one notification entry with event 'order.status.updated', orderId 'ORD-20250-0088', and status 'Delivered'.

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Alternative valid flow: status change from Pending to Processing generates report entry and in-app notification
classification_rationale: Covers an alternate valid status transition to verify reporting and notification visibility is consistent across different status changes.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0102 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-30078 (Gamma LLC)
API authentication token is valid for user ops_user_mlee with scope order:update
In-app notification service is active and configured for order status events
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250-0102/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/order-status?orderId=ORD-20250-0102
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?orderId=ORD-20250-0102&type=in_app
expected_result: PUT response returns HTTP 200 with status 'Processing'. GET /reports/order-status returns ORD-20250-0102 with status 'Processing', previous status 'Pending', updated_by 'ops_user_mlee', and timestamp 2025-07-15T09:15:00Z. GET /notifications returns an in-app notification record with orderId 'ORD-20250-0102', message containing 'Processing', and read_status 'false'.

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: ETL pipeline extracts status update and loads it into reporting data warehouse with correct dimensions
classification_rationale: Validates that the ETL process correctly propagates status updates from the transactional database into the reporting data warehouse.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0145 exists in the source transactional database with a status change from 'Processing' to 'Shipped' recorded at 2025-07-15T12:00:00Z
ETL job etl_order_status_sync is configured to run on a 5-minute schedule
Reporting data warehouse table dim_order_status is accessible and empty of records for ORD-20250-0145
ETL service account svc_etl has read access to source and write access to target
test_data: Source Record: {order_id: 'ORD-20250-0145', previous_status: 'Processing', new_status: 'Shipped', updated_by: 'ops_user_jdoe', updated_at: '2025-07-15T12:00:00Z'}
Target Table: dim_order_status in reporting warehouse
steps: Trigger the etl_order_status_sync job manually
Wait for the ETL job to complete with status 'SUCCESS'
Query the reporting warehouse: SELECT * FROM dim_order_status WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250-0145'
expected_result: ETL job etl_order_status_sync completes with status 'SUCCESS' and processes 1 record. The dim_order_status table contains a row for ORD-20250-0145 with new_status 'Shipped', previous_status 'Processing', updated_by 'ops_user_jdoe', updated_at '2025-07-15T12:00:00Z', and etl_load_timestamp within 1 minute of job completion.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Primarily happy-path coverage

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: UI report does not display status update when invalid non-existent order filter is applied
classification_rationale: Validates that reporting handles queries for non-existent orders gracefully without displaying false data.
preconditions: User account ops_user_jdoe exists with role Operations_Manager and has read permission on Order Status Report
Order ORD-99999-0000 does not exist in the system
Order Status Report is accessible at /reports/order-status
steps: Navigate to the Order Status Report at /reports/order-status
Enter 'ORD-99999-0000' in the Order ID filter field
Click the 'Apply Filter' button
expected_result: The Order Status Report displays the message 'No results found for Order ID: ORD-99999-0000'. The report table is empty with zero rows. No notification records are referenced.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Status update with disabled notification configuration does not generate notification but still appears in reporting
classification_rationale: Validates that reporting visibility is independent of notification configuration, and that missing notification config is handled gracefully.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0115 exists with current status 'Processing' for customer CUST-30090 (Delta Corp)
Notification configuration for customer CUST-30090 is disabled (notify_enabled: false)
API authentication token is valid for user ops_user_kwong with scope order:update
Reporting data warehouse is connected and consuming order status change events
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250-0115/status with the request body and authorization header
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/order-status?orderId=ORD-20250-0115
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?orderId=ORD-20250-0115
expected_result: PUT response returns HTTP 200 with a warning field: 'notification_skipped: Notification disabled for customer CUST-30090'. GET /reports/order-status returns ORD-20250-0115 with status 'Shipped' and timestamp 2025-07-15T11:45:00Z. GET /notifications returns an empty array for orderId ORD-20250-0115.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Unauthorised API request to update order status is rejected and does not generate reporting entry or notification
classification_rationale: Non-functional security test ensuring unauthorised status updates do not leak into reporting or notification systems.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0160 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-30099 (Zeta Ltd)
API token expired_token_2024 is an expired bearer token for user ops_user_expired
Reporting and notification services are running
steps: Send PUT request to /api/v1/orders/ORD-20250-0160/status with the expired authorization header and request body
Send GET request to /api/v1/reports/order-status?orderId=ORD-20250-0160
Send GET request to /api/v1/notifications?orderId=ORD-20250-0160&event=order.status.updated
expected_result: PUT response returns HTTP 401 with body {"error": "Unauthorized", "message": "Token is expired or invalid"}. GET /reports/order-status returns ORD-20250-0160 with status still 'Pending' and no record of a 'Shipped' transition. GET /notifications returns an empty array for the order.status.updated event on ORD-20250-0160.

ac_text: Status updates must be visible in reporting and notifications.
title: Database records status update with audit trail visible to reporting queries after rapid consecutive status changes
classification_rationale: Tests boundary behavior where multiple rapid status changes must all be individually visible in reporting data at the database level.
preconditions: Order ORD-20250-0130 exists with current status 'Pending' for customer CUST-30055 (Epsilon Inc)
Database order_status_history table is configured to capture all status transitions
Database connection is available with read access to order_status_history and notification_log tables
test_data: First Status Update: Pending → Processing at 2025-07-15T08:00:00Z by ops_user_jdoe
Second Status Update: Processing → Shipped at 2025-07-15T08:00:02Z by ops_user_jdoe
Third Status Update: Shipped → Delivered at 2025-07-15T08:00:04Z by ops_user_jdoe
Query: SELECT * FROM order_status_history WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250-0130' ORDER BY updated_at ASC
Notification Query: SELECT * FROM notification_log WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250-0130' ORDER BY created_at ASC
steps: Execute INSERT into order_status_history for ORD-20250-0130 transitioning from 'Pending' to 'Processing' at 2025-07-15T08:00:00Z
Execute INSERT into order_status_history for ORD-20250-0130 transitioning from 'Processing' to 'Shipped' at 2025-07-15T08:00:02Z
Execute INSERT into order_status_history for ORD-20250-0130 transitioning from 'Shipped' to 'Delivered' at 2025-07-15T08:00:04Z
Execute SELECT * FROM order_status_history WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250-0130' ORDER BY updated_at ASC
Execute SELECT * FROM notification_log WHERE order_id = 'ORD-20250-0130' ORDER BY created_at ASC
expected_result: The order_status_history query returns exactly 3 rows: row 1 with previous_status 'Pending', new_status 'Processing', updated_at 2025-07-15T08:00:00Z; row 2 with previous_status 'Processing', new_status 'Shipped', updated_at 2025-07-15T08:00:02Z; row 3 with previous_status 'Shipped', new_status 'Delivered', updated_at 2025-07-15T08:00:04Z. The notification_log query returns exactly 3 rows, one per status change, each with status 'sent' and matching timestamps.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result
